description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure unit featuring a set of rectangular, dark photovoltaic panels mounted on a low-profile, angular base. The panels are supported by a metallic frame, with visible red and gray structural elements that provide stability and possibly house electrical components. At one end, there is a small, box-like enclosure that may contain control electronics or monitoring equipment. The base appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and composite materials, with a slightly raised section and a sloped surface for optimal sunlight exposure. The overall design is modular and robust, suitable for installation in urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or small utility areas. The unit is designed to be stationary and is not intended for human entry or traversal.
description: A compact solar energy unit with dark photovoltaic panels on a sturdy, angular base, featuring red and gray structural elements and a small control enclosure. The panels are arranged for optimal sunlight capture and the design is robust for urban installation.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full rectangular array of solar panels and the layout of the red and gray base structure.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt of the solar panels and the small black enclosure at one end.
description_view_2: Opposite side view revealing the sloped base and the arrangement of the panels along the length of the structure.
description_view_3: End view focusing on the angular support structure and the alignment of the panels relative to the base.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with an aluminum frame and composite base
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of 2 rectangular panels
